extra.reasoning: The property G(opted_out implies not sent) holds. sent becomes true only via [t_send_allowed], whose guard is "not opted_out", so any state where sent is freshly set has opted_out=false. opted_out becomes true only via [t_optout], which in the same update sets sent:=false. Once opted_out=true it never reverts to false (no transition sets opted_out:=false). While opted_out=true: send_attempt matches only [t_send_blocked] (guard opted_out) since [t_send_allowed] is disabled, setting sent:=false; tick sets sent:=false; optout keeps sent:=false. Thus no transition can produce a state with opted_out=true and sent=true. The invariant holds initially (opted_out=false, sent=false) and is preserved by every transition (t_optout: opted_out=true,sent=false OK; t_send_allowed: opted_out=false OK; t_send_blocked: sent=false OK; t_tick: sent=false OK). Property holds across the entire reachable space, well within the 9-event bound.
